overview.summary: `workspace-runtime-command` owns explicit live E2E coverage for the command daemon contract across protocol smoke, core lifecycle, transcript-log recycling, local-OS-style sandbox operation parity, ephemeral workspace process-group behavior, externally killed process groups, invisible background subprocesses, and isolated workspace command cleanup. It exercises `sandbox.file.write`, `sandbox.file.edit`, `sandbox.file.read`, `sandbox.command.exec`, `sandbox.command.write_stdin`, `sandbox.command.poll`, `sandbox.command.collect_completed`, `sandbox.command.cancel`, `sandbox.command.count`, `sandbox.call.cancel`, and `sandbox.checkpoint.layer_metrics`. Module config lives at `crates/e2e-test/tests/workspace-runtime-command/config/default.test.yml`.

checklist.title: Lifecycle
checklist.description: `exec_command`, `write_stdin`, collect-completed, cancel, timeout, command count, and timestamped transcript behavior are coherent for running and completed commands.

checklist.title: Transcript Progress
checklist.description: Timestamped transcript progress remains observable through read_progress and operation-scoped stdin responses.

checklist.title: Transcript Recycling
checklist.description: PTY transcript logs are transient capture files and are removed after finite pre-yield completion, collect-completed finalization, cancellation, external process death, parallel prompt cleanup, and isolated command cancellation.

checklist.title: Cancel Kills Group
checklist.description: Command cancellation tears down the owned process group, drains commands, releases leases, and leaves no descendant marker processes.

checklist.title: Teardown Control Cancel
checklist.description: Ctrl-C (`\u0003`) and Ctrl-D (`\u0004`) stdin payloads both route to command cancel, drain the command, and recycle the transcript log.

checklist.title: Detached Child Contract
checklist.description: `nohup` and `setsid nohup` descendants have an explicit tested contract instead of an accidental leak policy.

checklist.title: Ephemeral Process Group
checklist.description: Ephemeral workspace commands stay running until all same-pgid subprocesses exit and collect exactly one terminal result.

checklist.title: Isolated Cleanup
checklist.description: Isolated workspace command cancellation and exit discard mode make same-port re-entry reusable.

checklist.title: Command Matrix
checklist.description: At least ten shell command families run through `exec_command` with multiple variants, foreground completion, changed-path assertions, and bounded trace/resource telemetry.

checklist.title: Prompt Read Cancel
checklist.description: Prompt-style stdin input, read_progress tail reads, collect-completed reads, and cancellation behave coherently without replaying consumed output.

checklist.title: Local Os Sandbox Parity
checklist.description: Local-OS-style write/edit/read, foreground/background command execution, stdin handoff, HTTP server observation, cancellation, and Ctrl-C/Ctrl-D teardown are covered through sandbox daemon ops.

checklist.title: Long Running Stderr Stdin
checklist.description: Long-lived commands that emit stderr, consume stdin, and then keep sleeping remain running and uncollectable until terminal action.

checklist.title: External Process Death
checklist.description: Externally signaled children and process groups finalize or become observable through `write_stdin` without leaking commands, leases, or marker processes.

checklist.title: Invisible Background Process
checklist.description: Silent redirected same-pgid subprocesses keep commands running even after the foreground command exits, preventing invisible background work from being missed.

checklist.title: Parallel Load
checklist.description: Commands execute under the configured `1/3/6/12` parallel ladder without leaked commands, leaked leases, unbounded upperdir/run-dir growth, or lost publishes.

checklist.title: Parallel Prompt Load
checklist.description: Prompt-style commands run at the configured `1/3/6/12` ladder with isolated stdin payloads, transcript-scoped replies, clean cancellation, and zero leaked commands or leases.

checklist.title: Negative Invalid
checklist.description: Nonzero exits, missing commands, unknown command ids, bogus stdin/cancel requests, and collect-completed misses return structured payloads without leaking commands or leases.

checklist.title: Output Backpressure
checklist.description: Large PTY output bursts stay timestamped in the transcript-backed stream and remain cancellable without leaked commands or leases.

checklist.title: Stdin Backpressure
checklist.description: Bounded stdin writes to non-reading consumers return promptly, leave the command cancellable, drain resources, and recycle transcript logs.

checklist.title: Isolated Prompt Parity
checklist.description: Isolated commands accept prompt input, support read_progress snapshots and collect-completed reads, cancel cleanly, and discard private command writes on isolated exit.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-protocol-smoke
test.description: Groups `commands_accept_stdin_and_release_on_cancel` and `commands_cancel_cleans_descendant_processes`: stdin echo, cancel cleanup, transcript recycling, lease release, and descendant process cleanup remain visible through the raw protocol.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-core-lifecycle
test.description: Groups `exec_returns_command_id`, `exec_command_outputs_timestamped_transcript_lines`, `write_stdin_echo`, `read_command_progress_returns_stateless_tail_snapshot`, `collect_completed_drains`, `collect_completed_preserves_full_timestamped_transcript`, `finite_exec_before_yield_recycles_transient_transcript_file`, `completed_command_removes_transcript_file`, `cancel_unblocks`, `command_count_accuracy`, `exec_timeout`, `output_burst_returns_full_timestamped_transcript`, `cancel_by_invocation_id_reports_already_done_for_idle_id`, `write_stdin_ctrl_d_removes_marker_process`, and `ctrl_c_char_cancels_command`: command handles, stdin, completion drain, finite pre-yield transcript recycling, long-running transcript removal, timeout, timestamped transcript output, read_progress snapshots, Ctrl-C/Ctrl-D cancellation, and cancel status remain coherent.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-output-and-detached-process-contract
test.description: Groups `nohup_child_keeps_command_running` and `setsid_nohup_contract`: plain `nohup` keeps the command running, and `setsid nohup` is accepted as an escaped detached child.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-ephemeral-workspace-process-group
test.description: Groups `exec_simple`, `lingering_child_keeps_command_running`, `command_completes_only_after_all_subprocesses_exit`, `cancel_kills_whole_command`, and `cancel_removes_lingering_descendant`: ephemeral workspace commands honor process-group completion, cancellation semantics, and transcript-log recycling.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-isolated-workspace-reentry
test.description: Uses `iws_same_port_discard`: an isolated command is cancelled, its transcript is recycled, exit discards the namespace state, and re-entering isolated mode can bind the same port cleanly.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-external-death-and-invisible-background
test.description: Groups `external_sigterm_child_finalizes_via_collect_completed`, `external_sigkill_process_group_is_observed_by_write_stdin`, and `silent_redirected_subprocess_keeps_command_running`: externally killed workloads finalize cleanly, recycle transcript logs, killed process groups are visible through stdin polling, and silent redirected same-pgid subprocesses keep commands running until explicit cancellation.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-command-matrix-and-load
test.description: Groups `command_matrix_covers_shell_families`, `stdin_prompt_progress_collect_and_cancel_variants`, and `parallel_command_matrix_load_stays_bounded`: one runner-visible matrix test covers at least ten command families with multiple variants, finite command transcript recycling, prompt/stdin transcript progress, collect-completed reads, cancellation, and the configured `1/3/6/12` parallel load ladder with resource bounds.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-local-os-sandbox-parity
test.description: Groups `local_os_sandbox_file_ops_write_edit_and_rejection`, `local_os_sandbox_command_foreground_background_and_stdin_handoff`, `local_os_sandbox_command_cancel_and_control_bytes`, and `local_os_sandbox_http_server_is_observed_and_cancelled`: the local-OS live coverage matrix is mirrored at the sandbox operation layer with sandbox-relative file paths, required edit arrays, direct file-op rejection, foreground command completion, background polling, command-id stdin handoff, HTTP observation, explicit cancellation, and Ctrl-C/Ctrl-D teardown.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-parallel-prompt-load
test.description: Uses `parallel_prompt_commands_ladder_stays_isolated_and_bounded`: prompt-style commands start at `1/3/6/12`, each worker receives its own stdin payload, read_progress snapshots do not replay consumed output, cancellation drains every command, transcript logs are recycled, and active leases return to zero.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-negative-invalid-and-backpressure
test.description: Groups `nonzero_exit_and_stderr_are_structured`, `stderr_and_stdin_output_keep_long_lived_command_running`, `missing_command_and_invalid_command_ids_are_structured`, `output_backpressure_preserves_utf8_and_drains_on_cancel`, and `stdin_to_non_reading_consumer_stays_bounded_and_cancellable`: nonzero shell exits, long-lived stderr/stdin commands, missing commands, unknown command ids, empty collect-completed reads, timestamped large output and UTF-8-safe transcript reads, bounded stdin pressure, post-pressure cancellation, transcript recycling, and resource drain all return coherent protocol payloads.

test.name: workspace-runtime-command-isolated-prompt-parity
test.description: Uses `iws_prompt_stdin_poll_cancel_private_discard`: an isolated prompt command accepts stdin, writes private isolated workspace content, read_progress snapshots do not replay consumed output, stays uncollected until cancellation, recycles its isolated transcript, and discards its private file after isolated exit.
